payload.text:
Refactor the provided index.html for Nkatanri Ltd.

Required Content Updates:

REMOVE ALL GINGER AND COCOA. The site must now be a 100% Hibiscus-focused specialist portal.

PRODUCTION NARRATIVE: "At Nkatanri, quality is produced, not just inspected. We oversee the drying and cleaning of our hibiscus flowers in Gaduwa District, Abuja".

COMPLIANCE: Change text to "Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification".

LOGISTICS: FOB Lagos is the only shipping term.

CONTACT: Remove "ISM" from the phone number. Update emails to sales@nkatanri.com and info@nkatanri.com.

**Refocusing on Hibiscus** I'm making good progress! I've started the refactor of `index.html` for Nkatanri Ltd. My primary task is to eliminate all ginger and cocoa mentions, transitioning the site to be 100% focused on Hibiscus. I'm carefully removing references and ensuring a smooth transition to the new focus. **Updating the Core Messaging** I've just revised the core messaging. I've updated the production narrative to emphasize quality control and specify our hibiscus processing location. I'm also updating the ISO certification status, shifting it from "Certified" to "Undergoing Certification". Furthermore, I've restricted shipping terms to "FOB Lagos." I'm also updating the contact details, and the phone number, removing any ISM references (or confirming their absence), and I am updating the email to sales@nkatanri.com and info@nkatanri.com. I'm now reviewing the `index.html` content. **Refining the Product Details** I'm now zeroing in on the product gallery section. I'm updating the intro text to align with the provided, detailed description of our production process. The grid will be adjusted to feature only Hibiscus, with centering or a single-column layout if necessary. I'm also addressing the "potency" description that mentions ginger. I'm also addressing the mentions of "The Food Basket" and "ISO 22000 Certified". **Verifying Shipping Details** I've checked the shipping terms and confirmed that "FOB Lagos" is the only option presented. I'm now examining the RFQ form, removing "Split Dried Ginger" from product interest selection. I'm updating the footer contact information to reflect the specified phone number. I'm also reviewing the product data and removing ginger-related entries from the `productsData` object. **Finalizing Content and Details** I'm now focusing on specific content refinements. I've updated the meta description to highlight our Hibiscus export focus and quality. I'm reviewing the "Hero Section" text, with slight adjustments to emphasize Hibiscus and keep "The Food Basket". The "Trust Section" now states "Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification." I'm also ensuring the product gallery intro aligns with the description, centering the Hibiscus card and removing the Ginger card. Finally, I'm verifying the phone number format and removing ginger data from the scripts and RFQ form. **Confirming Refinement Details** I'm now implementing the final content updates across all sections. I've removed all remaining "Ginger" references from the meta description, hero text, and the Trust Section, as planned. The product gallery now features the updated paragraph text. I've removed the Ginger product and updated the grid, and I am now ready to generate the final XML output for the changes. **Clarifying Structure and Scope** I'm now clarifying the overall scope. I realized `index.html` *contains* the full website markup, including content in the body, which *imports* `index.tsx`, so the site is not as I expected. Therefore, I need to update the `App.tsx` and components to match the changes to the content. I will also be ensuring that "Hibiscus" takes center stage across all sections, and the logistics focus is FOB only. The RFQ should have the select options, and I will be cleaning the `productsData` in the script. **Analyzing the Architecture** I'm now analyzing the architecture implied by the files. `index.html`'s structure directly contradicts the presence of `index.tsx`, as the HTML doesn't include the expected `
`. However, I'll proceed with modifying the HTML based on the user's explicit request to refactor the provided `index.html` file. My goal is to work within the provided files. **Confirming Implementation Focus** I've clarified the build approach. The key is now explicitly refactoring the provided static `index.html` file, not a hypothetical React app. I will focus on the static HTML, even if it might not be the complete implementation. I've reconfirmed the prompt's instruction to refactor the `index.html` file, despite the presence of React files, and I am prioritizing the HTML file edits. **Defining the Scope** I've made a key realization. The existing `index.html` file seems to be the content source, as it presents the currently displayed information. My focus is now on meticulously updating this static HTML file, which the user explicitly requested, to align with the revised content. I'll prioritize these HTML modifications, addressing the meta description, title, navigation, and hero sections as requested. **Updating the HTML Content** I'm now diving into the HTML, starting with the content updates. I've updated the "Hero Section" to "Premium Hibiscus Export Hub" and "Explore Produce" to "Explore Hibiscus." I've updated the Trust Section to "Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification." I'm also ensuring the narrative text in the product gallery is updated to reflect the hibiscus focus, and the ginger element is removed. I am also updating the logistics table with updated headers. **Reviewing Contact Details** I'm now focusing on the contact details within the `index.html` file. I've verified the phone number is now correctly listed as +234 810 773 8111, and the emails are accurate as sales@nkatanri.com and info@nkatanri.com. I've confirmed that "ISM" is not present in the provided HTML. I'm also considering the need to update `metadata.json` to reflect changes. **Preparing the XML Output** I'm now generating the XML output based on the changes. I've updated the meta description, Trust Section, and product gallery per the edits to `index.html`. Specifically, the meta description now highlights the "Premium B2B Hibiscus Export Hub". I've updated the Trust section to "Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification." The product gallery now features the updated descriptive text. I am also adjusting the select options within the RFQ form. ```xml Nigeria's Premium B2B Hibiscus Export Hub. Hands-on producers of Grade A Dried Hibiscus. Quality from farm-gate to export bag. Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification At Nkatanri, quality is produced, not just inspected. We oversee the drying and cleaning of our hibiscus flowers in Gaduwa District, Abuja. Hibiscus ``` **Refining Shipping and Product Data** I'm now integrating the updated shipping term and product data within the HTML. The table row clearly displays "Standard" shipping, referencing "FOB Lagos". The product select options now only include "Dried Hibiscus Flower" and "Other". I am now focusing on the product data within the scripts. I am also planning the wording for the ISO update, which must be concise. ```xml FOB Lagos ``` **Updating the Trust Section** I'm now zeroing in on the Trust Section in `index.html`. I am updating the "ISO 22000 Certified" heading to reflect the "Undergoing Certification" status. I'm also ensuring the meta description in `metadata.json` matches the updates to `index.html`. Finally, I've re-examined the footer contact information, confirming its accuracy and completeness. ```xml Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification ``` **Clarifying File Interactions** I'm now clarifying the relationship between files, given the focus on `index.html`. It appears `index.html` might not *use* any React components directly, so `ProductGallery.tsx` and similar files may be unused. Since the user asked to refactor the *provided* `index.html`, I'll stick to updating that file, and I won't modify the React files. The content of `index.html` is very close to the rendered webpage. **Confirming HTML-Only Approach** I've confirmed my approach: the provided `index.html` is a static file, and I'll focus solely on updating it. I'm ignoring the React components since they are not used. I'll maintain the `index.html` file as the content source, and update the meta description, Trust Section, and product gallery per the edits to it. My focus remains on this static HTML, and I will be returning *only* the `index.html` file (and `metadata.json` for description updates). ```xml Nkatanri - Premium B2B Hibiscus Export

Premium Hibiscus Export Hub

Quality Dried Hibiscus Flowers for Export

Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification

Our commitment to quality is always improving.

Our Product

At Nkatanri, quality is produced, not just inspected. We oversee the drying and cleaning of our hibiscus flowers in Gaduwa District, Abuja.

Shipping

Term Detail
Shipping FOB Lagos

Request a Quote

Contact us: +234 810 773 8111 | sales@nkatanri.com | info@nkatanri.com

 ``` **Refining the HTML Structure** I'm now integrating the additional changes I've made into the HTML structure. I've updated the `title` tag to "Nkatanri - Hibiscus Specialists" and reviewed the navigation to match the updated sections. I'm also now adjusting the meta description based on the provided changes, and preparing the XML output. I'm focusing on the "Home", "Hibiscus", and "Contact" nav links. ```xml Nkatanri - Hibiscus Specialists
Nkatanri
Home Hibiscus Contact

Premium Hibiscus Export

Quality Dried Hibiscus Flowers for Export

Our Hibiscus

At Nkatanri, quality is produced, not just inspected. We oversee the drying and cleaning of our hibiscus flowers in Gaduwa District, Abuja.

Undergoing ISO 22000 Certification

Our commitment to quality is always improving.

Shipping

Term Detail
Shipping FOB Lagos

entries.description: Refactor to 100% Hibiscus focus; update meta description, hero text, production narrative, compliance status, product grid (remove ginger), RFQ options, and modal data.
